commentaries.text: Real Madrid take a 0-1 lead thanks to Arda Guler.
Bayern Munich are pushing forward but Harry Kane's finish is wide of the goal.

Corner awarded to Bayern Munich.
Goal! Bayern Munich draw level to 1-1 through a Aleksandar Pavlovic header.
Great play from Joshua Kimmich to set up the goal.

That's a fine assist from Vinicius Junior.

Bayern Munich make their second substitution with Jamal Musiala replacing Serge Gnabry.

Michael Olise is on target to give Bayern Munich a 4-3 advantage.
Harry Kane instrumental with a fine assist.
Arda Guler (Real Madrid) gets a yellow card after the match has ended!

Referee Slavko Vincic blows his whistle to start this pivotal 9:00 PM UEFA Champions League quarterfinal at the Allianz Arena.
Bayern Munich step onto the pitch holding a vital aggregate advantage after they secured a 2-1 away victory over their Spanish rivals earlier this month.
Vincent Kompany's squad carry exceptional momentum tonight, riding a flawless five-match European winning streak and sitting comfortably in second place in the seasonal rankings.
Alvaro Arbeloa sets up Real Madrid in a highly offensive 4-3-3 formation as the visitors urgently chase the game to overturn the deficit.
Kylian Mbappe aims to add to his staggering fourteen seasonal goals to spark a comeback, while Harry Kane leads the home attack hoping to build on his impressive eleven-goal tally.
GOAL! Arda Guler rifles a left-footed strike into the net from 35 meters! The midfielder records his first goal of the season to give Real Madrid an early 1-0 lead in this massive fixture.
Kane looks for an instant response as he letches onto the ball in the half-space, but his left-footed strike from 30 meters out clears the target. Real Madrid maintain their early lead.
Kimmich prepares the delivery from the corner flag as Bayern Munich search for an immediate equalizer after that shock opening goal.
PAVLOVIC! Bayern Munich level the score at 1-1 as the midfielder directs a header home from 10 meters! Joshua Kimmich provides a perfect delivery for Pavlovic to claim his first goal of the season.
Upamecano feeds Michael Olise in the final third. Olise unleashes a powerful left-footed drive from 35 meters out on the wing, but a defensive touch diverts the effort wide of the target.
Kimmich approaches the flag to deliver another set-piece, searching for a second successful assist as Bayern Munich maintain high pressure in the final third.
Kylian Mbappe is flagged for offside after drifting into the half-space. The Real Madrid attacker mistimed his run while looking to breach the defense 15 meters from the goal.
Kylian Mbappé creates a massive opening in a central lane and strikes from 10 meters with his right foot, but his effort rockets high over the crossbar!
Michael Olise prepares to deliver the set-piece as Bayern Munich seek to reclaim their lead and capitalize on this pressure.
Joshua Kimmich fires a right-footed shot from 20 meters in the half-space, but the attempt takes a deflection and loops harmlessly wide and over the goal frame.
Olise steps up to swing this corner in, aiming to turn this sustained pressure into a crucial lead for the Bavarians!
Luis Diaz drifts behind the backline on the wing, roughly 25 meters from goal. The assistant referee flags the winger for offside, interrupting the Bayern Munich attack.
Olise strikes from the half-space following a feed from Kimmich, but the 20-meter effort clips a defender and rises over the woodwork.
Kimmich readies the cross from the flag, eyeing his second assist of the evening to give Munich the advantage.
Michael Olise readies a cross from the corner arc, seeking to exploit the same dead-ball opening that saw Bayern level the score earlier.
Kimmich latches on to Olise’s pass in the half-space and rifles a left-footed attempt from 20 meters, but the strike toward the left side of the target is saved.
Kimmich prepares to deliver from the quadrant as Bayern Munich look to exploit their set-piece dominance and break this deadlock.
Vinicius Junior bursts into the heart of the area and hammers a shot from five meters, but it is saved!
Real Madrid win a free-kick in a lethal central pocket. The ball rests just 15 meters from the target.

Guler strikes a clinical left-footed free-kick into the top right corner! Real Madrid lead 2-1 after this sensational effort from a 20-meter distance, marking the youngster's second goal of the season.
Josip Stanisic receives a yellow card for dissent, marking his second booking of the tournament as Bayern Munich trail three-two in this frantic opening half.

Josip Stanisic finds Serge Gnabry in a central pocket. Gnabry strikes a left-footed effort from 20 meters, sending the ball low toward the bottom right, but the attempt is turned away by the goalkeeper.
Stanisic unleashes a stinging right-footed attempt 20 meters out in the half-space, but his shot inside the right post is parried by the goalkeeper.

KANE! The forward receives from Dayot Upamecano and directs a low right-footed finish into the bottom right corner from 10 meters! This marks his 12th goal of the campaign as Bayern Munich claw back to make it 2-2!
Eder Militao enters the referee’s notebook for serious foul play, recording his second caution of the season as Real Madrid remain level at two-all in this high-stakes quarterfinal.
Vinicius Junior connects with Arda Guler's pass in the half-space and launches a right-footed strike at a 20-meter range, though the blast sails high above the frame.
Mbappe restores the Real Madrid lead! Vinicius Junior supplies the delivery, and Mbappe steers a right-footed shot into the lower left from five meters for his 15th goal this campaign.

Real Madrid hold a 3-2 lead over Bayern Munich at halftime following a remarkably open and offensive 45 minutes.
Arda Guler stunned the home crowd with a spectacular brace, forcing the hosts to constantly chase the game despite crucial equalizers from Aleksandar Pavlovic and Harry Kane.
Bayern generated relentless attacking volume and racked up seven corners, but they must urgently adjust their defensive structure to contain Kylian Mbappe and a ruthless Madrid frontline.
Bayern Munich bring on Alphonso Davies for Josip Stanisic to inject speed into their wide play. Stanisic exits after being cautioned earlier in this high-stakes quarterfinal clash.

The second half is underway at the Allianz Arena! Real Madrid hold a 3-2 advantage following a breathless first forty-five minutes of Champions League quarter-final action. Bayern Munich emerge from the break with a swift adjustment. Alphonso Davies replaces Josip Stanisic, who collected a yellow card earlier in the match.

Bayern must chase the game on home soil to find an equalizer. Davies' introduction provides immediate width and blistering pace down the flank. Real Madrid command a favorable position to absorb the pressure. The visitors will aim to defend their lead while punishing any defensive gaps on the counter-attack.
Luis Diaz finds space in a central pocket 15 meters out and unleashes a right-footed strike, but a deflection sends the ball spinning wide of the left post.
Joshua Kimmich steps up to the corner flag, searching for the delivery that might restore parity for the Munich side.
Upamecano connects with Kimmich’s delivery in the heart of the penalty area, yet the defender sends his strike over the bar from 5 meters!
Alexander-Arnold stands over the ball at the corner flag, looking to provide a delivery that could decisively widen the margin for Real Madrid in this quarterfinal clash!
Kylian Mbappe pounces on the service from Trent Alexander-Arnold and lashes a right-footed effort from 10 meters, but it is saved at the left post!
Alexander-Arnold marches toward the quadrant, poised to swing in a set-piece that could put the visitors in total control.
Alexander-Arnold whips a cross into the box for Jude Bellingham; he connected from ten meters, but the effort was saved!

Luis Diaz is caught beyond the backline on the flank, and the assistant’s flag quickly ends Bayern’s latest surge. The Colombian mistimed his run roughly 25 meters out, stifling the hosts' momentum immediately after Jamal Musiala’s tactical introduction.
Jamal Musiala joins the action for Bayern Munich, replacing Serge Gnabry as the home side looks to find a route back into this match.
Eduardo Camavinga enters the match for Real Madrid, replacing Brahim Diaz. This tactical change adds defensive presence to their midfield as they look to preserve their one-goal lead.
Vinicius Junior connects with a pass from Mbappe and strikes from 15 meters, but the left-footed attempt sailed high and wide of the target.
Kylian Mbappe finds Federico Valverde in a central pocket 20 meters from goal. Valverde unleashes a left-footed strike aimed toward the right of the frame, but the effort is saved.
Olise manufactures space in a central pocket 20 meters from goal and lashes a stinging left-footed strike. The goalkeeper denies his high-rising attempt to maintain Madrid's one-goal advantage.
Kimmich positions himself at the corner flag as Bayern Munich seek a vital equalizer to keep their Champions League ambitions alive.
Davies strikes from 20 meters in the half-space, seeing his left-footed attempt diverted wide via a deflection.
Antonio Rudiger goes into the referee's book for serious foul play, recording his second caution of the season while Real Madrid hold a narrow 3-2 lead.
Eduardo Camavinga lashes a left-footed strike from 30 meters through the middle of the pitch, only for a crucial deflection to carry his attempt wide.
Upamecano connects with Olise’s delivery 10 meters out, but the defender steered his attempt high and wide to the right.
Michael Olise meets a pass from Harry Kane 20 meters out and strikes with his left foot, but the shot lacks the necessary dip and rises over the woodwork.
Eduardo Camavinga earns a yellow card for serious foul play, marking the midfielder's second caution of the European campaign as Real Madrid cling to a narrow 3-2 lead.
Slavko Vincic halts play while medical personnel enter to attend to an injury. Real Madrid maintain their lead in this quarterfinal as the action pauses for treatment.

Eduardo Camavinga sees red! The midfielder receives another booking for unsporting behavior and is sent for an early shower. Real Madrid must navigate the final stages with ten men while clinging to a 3-2 lead. It is a moment of madness from the Frenchman that leaves the visitors vulnerable.

LUIS DIAZ! The Colombian drags Bayern Munich level with a magnificent right-footed strike from 20 meters! Jamal Musiala creates the opening, and Diaz clinical finish marks his sixth goal this campaign to make it 3-3!
Franco Mastantuono replaces Arda Guler as Real Madrid look to stabilize their shape after the recent equalizer. Guler departs following a clinical two-goal performance with the score level at 3-3.
Thiago Pitarch Pinar replaces Trent Alexander-Arnold as Real Madrid prioritizes defensive stability. The visitors look to see out these final moments with ten men following the late chaos.

OLISE! Michael Olise hammers the Bavarians back into the lead, latching on to a Harry Kane pass to blast a left-footed shot high into the net from 15 meters! He records his fourth goal of this term and secures a 4-3 advantage in the final moments!
Arda Guler picks up a caution for dissent as tempers flare following the conclusion of Real Madrid's 4-3 defeat.

Arda Guler receives a second booking for his verbal protests after the referee blew the whistle. The Madrid attacker, despite finding the net twice tonight, is dismissed for backchat towards Slavko Vincic. Real Madrid's discipline crumbles as the youngster sees red after the conclusion of play.

Bayern Munich claim a breathless 4-3 victory against Real Madrid to secure their spot in the Champions League semifinals!
Arda Guler scored a spectacular first-half brace to keep the visitors in front, but Eduardo Camavinga earned a costly red card in the 86th minute to derail their momentum.
Luis Diaz instantly punished the ten men with a clinical strike from distance before Michael Olise converted Harry Kane's stoppage-time pass to complete a monumental comeback.
Dani Ceballos receives a yellow card for dissent from the bench, highlighting the fraying nerves within the Real Madrid camp while trailing 4-3.

insights.text: Bayern Munich have been one of the most effective teams in the UEFA Champions League this season, averaging 3 goals per match, which is above the league average of 1.5 goals per match.
In the last 3 matches of the UEFA Champions League, Bayern Munich scored 12 goals (average 4) while Real Madrid scored 6 (average 2).
Bayern Munich seem to be in a good form. They won 3 out of the last 3 matches of the UEFA Champions League.
In their last 5 UEFA Champions League matches, Bayern Munich have received an average of 2.6 cards per match, while Real Madrid have averaged 1.4 cards.
Chances are good to see a goal from Bayern Munich within the first half. They have averaged 1.5 goals before halftime  in the UEFA Champions League this season.
Bayern Munich scored at least one goal per match in the UEFA Champions League this season.
Bayern Munich shine in defence lately. They conceded less than one goal per match on average in the last 5 matches of the UEFA Champions League.
In their last 3 UEFA Champions League matches, Bayern Munich have been awarded an average of 8 corners per match, while Real Madrid have averaged 5 corners.
Bayern Munich are leading by a goal after 90+5 minutes of the match.In their last 4 similar situations in the UEFA Champions League, they've won 4 times.
In their last 5 UEFA Champions League  matches, Bayern Munich have received an average of 0.6 cards in the first half per match, while Real Madrid have averaged 0.2 cards.
In the last 10 matches of the UEFA Champions League, Bayern Munich conceded on average 1 goals per match while Real Madrid conceded on average 1.5 goals per match.

facts.statement: During the last 9 meetings with Bayern Munich playing at home, Bayern Munich have won 3 times, there have been 3 draws while Real Madrid have won 3 times. The goal difference is 13-10 in favour of Real Madrid.
During the last 19 meetings, Bayern Munich have won 6 times, there have been 4 draws while Real Madrid have won 9 times. The goal difference is 28-23 in favour of Real Madrid.
Bayern Munich's last home win against Real Madrid was in 2015.
Bayern Munich have a winning streak of 4 matches.
Bayern Munich haven't lost in their last 14 games.
Bayern Munich have won 6 home matches in a row.
The result of the first leg: Real Madrid - Bayern Munich 1-2.
Bayern Munich have scored at least one goal for 49 consecutive matches.
Real Madrid have conceded a goal in each of their last 6 matches.
Real Madrid have scored at least one goal for 8 consecutive matches.
Bayern Munich wins 1st half in 65% of their matches, Real Madrid in 42% of their matches.
Bayern Munich wins 65% of halftimes, Real Madrid wins 42%.
The winner of their last meeting was Bayern Munich.
On their last meeting Bayern Munich won by 1 goals.
When Bayern Munich leads 1-0 at home, they win in 91% of their matches.
When Real Madrid leads 0-1 away, they win in 90% of their matches.
When Bayern Munich is down 0-1 home, they win 100% of their matches.
When Real Madrid is down 1-0 away, they win 9% of their matches.
In UEFA Champions League, Bayern Munich has better performance than Real Madrid.
Bayern Munich's performance of the last 5 matches is better than Real Madrid's.
Bayern Munich have lost just 1 of their last 5 games against Real Madrid (in all competitions).
In the last 5 meetings Bayern Munich won 2, Real Madrid won 1, 2 draws.
Real Madrid have lost just 1 of their last 5 UEFA Champions League games against Bayern Munich.
Bayern Munich have won just 1 of their last 5 UEFA Champions League games against Real Madrid.
Bayern Munich scores 2 goals in a match against Real Madrid and Real Madrid scores 1.6 goals against Bayern Munich (on average).
Average number of goals in meetings between Bayern Munich and Real Madrid is 3.6.
Average number of goals in the first half in meetings between Bayern Munich and Real Madrid is 1.
Bayern Munich scores 3.74 goals when playing at home and Real Madrid scores 2.06 goals when playing away (on average).
If Kylian Sanmi Mbappe Lottin scores against Bayern Munich it will be his 15th goal for Real Madrid this season.
If Antonio Rudiger receives a yellow card in today's match, he will be suspended after collecting 2 yellow cards so far this season.
This was Kylian Sanmi Mbappe Lottin's 15th goal for the season.
The current draw (4-4) on aggregate is enough for Real Madrid to reach the next round on away goals.
4-3 in today's game is enough for Bayern Munich to go through to the next round, by leading 6-4 on aggregate.
Bayern Munich goes through to the next round by winning 6-4 on aggregate.
